3: <header>1. Introduction </header>

Today, increasing the humans population and the activity caused by different industrial fields, transportation, agriculture and etc, lead to increasing the concentration of green houses to excess value in the atmosphere which is lead to greenhouse impacts and incidence climate change such as global warming and the destruction of ozone layer (Vitousek et al., 1997). If climate change trends continue, the people of the world will face with significant changes such as melting of polar ice, rising the water of sea level, changes in type of atmospheric precipitation, extreme storms, decreasing in value of agriculture production by frostbite, hail, freezing, drought, flood, erosion, flooding in agriculture lands, changes of seasons precipitation and also destruction of biological, plant and animal diversity (Peters and Darling, 1985). So increasing of earth temperature has different impacts and implication in different perspective (Mitchell, 2003).
In recent years, attention to environmental problem has been

increased. By increasing the knowledge of environment, different part of economic has been busy on evaluating of environmental impacts of their own activities (Nemecek et al., 2008). Agriculture is one of the important parts of economic which has a lot of impacts on environment (Hongmin et al., 2008). During the 20th century, the agricultural products has increased dramatically which the increasing of mechanization, improving the production method, extensive use of fertilizer and pesticide and advances in animal husbandry has helped a lot to increasing the production. But increasing the production and extensive use of inputs lead to kind of environmental problem (Brentrup et al., 2004a). Greenhouse gases has different heating impacts on world climate because the molecular weight and the ability to retain the heat and their life is different in atmosphere (EPA, 2009). The GWP idea which is developed by the Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC), is compared the ability of each greenhouse gas in trapping the heat in atmosphere relative to some reference gases (Holden and Hoyer, 2005). GWP is considered as the indicator for measuring the value of particle of greenhouse gases which is effective in global warming. This is a relative scale which calculated in a certain time distance (IPCC, 2006). CO2 is a reference gas with

GWP value by 1. The GWP values for methane and nitrogen oxide are 21 and 310 respectively in a period of 100 years (Lo et al., 2005). Table 1, is indicative of GWP value of some pollutant. Gases emis- sion is reported on GWP weight equivalent of CO2. Methane and nitrogen oxide usually are reported equivalent of carbon dioxide and using of weight indicators. The equivalent of carbon dioxide is carbon dioxide emission value which in the specific time period as an emission value from one greenhouse gas with long life lead to value to global warming. The equivalent of carbon dioxide is ob- tained by multiply the emission of one greenhouse gas in GWP value (Change, 2006) (see Table 2).

The greenhouse gases are the gases which is trapping the heat in atmosphere. Greenhouse gases usually are water vapor, CO2, CH4, N20, O3 (Mitchell, 1989). The water vapor of major gas is the climate control but the emission of water vapor from the fossil fuel forms the very low proportion of global evaporation in world (about 0.0013 percent) (Delucchi, 2003). Natural processes constantly spread or removed the CO2 gases, CH4 and N20 from atmosphere but the humans activity can reduce or increase greenhouse gases in atmosphere and changes the world average (Houghton, 2009). Ozone won't spread direct but as the result, the chemical reactions which are including carbon monoxide (CO), nitrogen oxides (NOx) and hydrocarbons (HC) is produced (EPA, 2009).

Poultry industry is in the order of the largest and most devel- oped of existing industries in the agriculture sector and by increasing the population, increasing the income level and people welfare and as the result increasing the demand for protein prod- ucts, extension and development of poultry industry seams necessary (Etches, 1998). The activities of breeding the laying hen is very important, because the main product and the final production of these category of poultry is the edible egg which has a significant share as an important protein and nutritious product in supplying a part of animal protein which is in the society required (Barbut, 2001).

According to research conducted in the United States, 4.6 percent of greenhouse gas emissions in the agricultural industry, which is approximately 37 percent owned by the production of beef, 5.11% for maintenance and milk production of dairy cows, 4.4% maintenance and production of pork and poultry was 0.06 percent. The amount of greenhouse gas emissions for poultry production appears low compared to other livestock industry, In this way, The life cycle and supply chain of poultry production as a source of greenhouse gas emissions are negligible appointed, But under- standing how and at what stages in poultry production is the largest amount of greenhouse gas emissions is important.
Agriculture and Animal Husbandry Industry must devise and management of resources and environmental impact, improve performance in key areas such as water use, energy consumption

and environmental pollution.

The researchers used the method of LCA to assess the environ- mental impact of crop production and horticulture and the milk production in Iran have done research, But in the production of eggs, this research has been done the first time.

According to Decker and collaborate on research with non-cage hens organic conducted in the Netherlands in 2008, Greenhouse gas emissions to produce 1 kg of organic eggs 4 kg equivalent CO2 was determined that emissions of oxides of nitrogen (N20) with a share of 77 percent, Carbon dioxide (CO2) 21 percent and methane (CH4) 3 percent was estimated that production of poultry feed is the most important factor in the release of the index, also the two main factors in energy consumption were feed production (62 percent) and transportation (33%).

Cederberg et al., 2009 in Sweden surveyed on identifying environmental maintenance egg production systems and multiple production (38% of the cage, the cage 56 percent no and 6% organically). Laying hens in the poultry kept for 72 weeks and ac- cording to reports, around 300 eggs per hen were produced. Global warming emissions to produce 1 kg of eggs were calculated 4/1 kg equivalent of CO2, that share of each gas N20, CO2 and CH4 respectively 56 percent, 39 percent and 4 percent, with 85 percent of the emissions related has been producing chicken feed (Cederberg et al., 2009).

Wiedemann and McGahan study carried out on Environmental Impact Assessment one kg of egg production chain using LCA in 2011. Greenhouse gas emissions was declared 3.1 kg eq CO2 for cage system and 1.6 kg eq CO2 for without cage, that preparation of feed for laying hens will have the greatest impact on emissions. The amount of energy to produce 1 kg of eggs in cage systems and without cage was calculated respectively 7.10 and 1.3 Mj, that preparation of feed has the highest energy consumption (Wiedemann and McGahan, 2011).

According to Decker and collaborate on research with non-cage hens organic conducted in the Netherlands in 2008, Greenhouse gas emissions to produce 1 kg of organic eggs 4 kg equivalent CO2 was determined that emissions of oxides of nitrogen (N20) with a share of 77 percent, Carbon dioxide (CO2) 21 percent and methane (CH4) 3 percent was estimated that production of poultry feed is the most important factor in the release of the index, also the two main factors in energy consumption were feed production (62 percent) and transportation (33%).

Cederberg et al., 2009 in Sweden surveyed on identifying environmental maintenance egg production systems and multiple production (38% of the cage, the cage 56 percent no and 6% organically). Laying hens in the poultry kept for 72 weeks and ac- cording to reports, around 300 eggs per hen were produced. Global warming emissions to produce 1 kg of eggs were calculated 4/1 kg equivalent of CO2, that share of each gas N20, CO2 and CH4 respectively 56 percent, 39 percent and 4 percent, with 85 percent of the emissions related has been producing chicken feed (Cederberg et al., 2009).

Wiedemann and McGahan study carried out on Environmental Impact Assessment one kg of egg production chain using LCA in 2011. Greenhouse gas emissions was declared 3.1 kg eq CO2 for cage system and 1.6 kg eq CO2 for without cage, that preparation of feed for laying hens will have the greatest impact on emissions. The amount of energy to produce 1 kg of eggs in cage systems and without cage was calculated respectively 7.10 and 1.3 Mj, that preparation of feed has the highest energy consumption (Wiedemann and McGahan, 2011).

14: The study area for units of laying hen, considered in Alborz province which has 5833 km2 extent. Also this province has located in the slopes of the Alborz mountains and has mild climate and the average of annual temperature is 16 °C and has 1300 m of the height of sea level and traditionally this place considered as agri- culture cradle and animal husbandry of Iran because of having the mild climate. Alborz province has the important and significant role in agriculture activities field because of the certain geographical position, suitable climate condition and Tehran's neighborhood in industrial production and agriculture production market and also scientific research. The formation of the first livestock, poultry and laying hen farms, institutions of country's incubation in this prov- ince beside the hardworking and pioneer producer play the main role in a development of agriculture and livestock and poultry in- dustry. Despite of the development of urbanity and emigration to this province, still the Alborz province deserve to have a honor in position of agriculture and animal husbandry as the large share of scientific and research institution of ministry of agriculture has located in Alborz province (Anonymous, 2013).
15: The life cycle assessment (LCA) at first has developed as an environmental tool to apply in industry in 1960 in Europe (Guinée, 2002). In recent years, LCA was used in agriculture sector in response to growing information demand about produce the pro- duction and its supply chain (Roy et al., 2009). Using this process in agricultural sector was faced with some complication because of the dynamic and open agriculture systems which this led to develop some methods for ensuring of detailed assessment (Gliessman, 1990). The life cycle assessment (LCA) considered the environmental feature in whole life cycle of one product of pro- cessed materials to production, consumption, the end of biological actions and final disposal. There are 4 stages of one LCA study including the stage of defining the purpose and application scope, analyze stage and assessment and consequence stage and inter- preting stage (ISO, 2006).
16: The goal for this study was to develop an LCA model to study impact assessment from the production of egg in Alborz, which may aid in environmentally conscious decision-making. The scope included two separate stages. The first stage was to calculate the environmental impact for production of three main crop in feed for chickens, and the second was the egg production, where egg is produced.
17: The following information were collected from the poultry of laying hen of 5 province of Alborz province (Karaj, Savojbolagh,

NazarAbad, Talequivalenthan and Eshtehard). This study was the survey and life cycle assessment of egg production of 420 days of period for the thousands of chickens. The number of poultry hens in Alborz Province were 98, that only 52 farms were active and ongoing activities and the rest by the lack of experienced birder and also non-economic for poultry owners were inactive. The present data were collected from 52 farms in Alborz province. The present study have several stages which at first details of consumable ele- ments (including poultry farming capacity, the amount of elec- tricity consumption, diesel fuel, natural gas, feed consumption, etc.), also information related to beekeeping equipment and data output from poultry, including eggs, chicken removed and manure produced was filled by questionnaires and interviews with au- thorities of livestock and poultry area.
18: After the interest classification data, a unit was considered as performance unit, performance unit is a reference which is linking the input and output of a product together and also it's a unit which can compare different systems based on common structure (Wiedemann and McGahan, 2011). Also the performance unit basis which all computation is carried out during the life cycle of product (Nemecek et al., 2011a, 2011b). In this study data entered into the software based on 1000 chicken during the 420 days. But for the more obvious results of environmental indicators that were output software, we calculated them on the basis of 1 kg of eggs during this period.
19: To perform the life cycle assessment of egg in pullets period of SimaPro software which design and developed for this purpose is used (PRé Consultants, 2003). The SimaPro software stored primal information in database, For example, electricity, inputs that were used in the production of eggs. The software is stored within the database of various ways to produce electricity. So electricity con- sumption in poultry was selected as an input that manufacturing method was similar to the method used to produce electricity in the region. Finally, to calculate the environmental indicators were selected as a model among models within the application that are listed with description and History and use cases. To calculate environmental indicators (warming potential, acidification, Eutro- phication, toxicity, etc.) crops and livestock was used CML-IA baseline V3.01/EU25 model.
20: The egg production system has considered when the laying pullets were spread in poultry. For egg production the inputs like land and equipment, labor force, electricity, fossil fuel, natural gasoline, consumption water, poultry feed were taken. The most input which is used during this period was diet food. So the pro- duction of 3 main of hen diet (corn, wheat and soybean) has been examined from the first, production in farm, harvesting and car- rying to silos then from silos to poultry stock.
The mentioned inputs into poultry were used and finally egg, the national chicken and Guano (chicken manure) were considered as output in interest period of 420 days in this systems.
21: One of important and necessary proceedings in LCA study is choosing the systems boundary and determined which activities present in LCA study. The life cycle assessment is cradle to grave

attitude but there is possibility in order to more concentrate on processes, the system boundary has considered as part of the whole process and the results has selected based on boundary and expressed for a smaller scale (Khoshnevisan et al., 2014; McDougall, 2001). Boundary system of egg production in this research is such that the process production combines three products in the diet (corn, soybeans and wheat) were analyzed separately. The result output of the three product that was stored in software, as one of the farm inputs the name of diet was used the desired compound. So in this study, system boundaries was taken the inputs in feed production (three crops) in farms and then inputs in poultry (as feed, electricity, fuel and etc.) and finally desired output (eggs).

A summary of the environmental impacts, assessed on the basis
of egg production (per kg egg in period of 420 days for 1000 chicken) is shown in Table 4.

The value of global warming during the whole production stages of one kg of egg was calculated 4/07 kg equivalent of CO2, which the most important of emission reasons of this indicator according to Fig. 3 is the feed production for layer hens. Due to the investigation which has done by Dekker et al. (2008) in Netherland, the value of global warming for produce one kg of organic egg has estimated by 4 kg equivalent of CO2 which the chicken feed product was the most important factor in value of emission of this indicator. In another study, Cederberg et al. (2009) in Netherland has worked on recognition of environmental focus of egg production, the value of global warming calculated for the production of 1 kg of egg 1.4 CO2 equivalent also the diet production has the most effect on the emission value of this indicator.

For one kg of egg production in Alborz, 1/22 X 10 g of CFC-11 pollutant enter to environment which can lead to destruction of ozone layer in stratosphere. The feed production and electricity consumption and diesel fuel has the most effect on value of this indicator for produce this product in Alborz. Chlorofluorocarbons are the most important material which has the destruction feature of ozone layer (Guinée, 2002). Destruction of ozone layer lead the effects like skin cancer, accession the molecular damage to mate- rial, damage to plant and animal that happen because of the increasing of ultraviolet (Bare, 2002, 2011).

Also the environmental pro- tection agency of US (EPA) has introduced the PARA DI COLORIZEN as a third effective group in cancer in human (National toxicology program, 1987). To produce each kg of egg production, photo- chemical oxidation potential (smog) 2/05 g equivalent of C2H2 were spread to environment, which the diet production including soy- bean production has the most share in value of this indicator in interest producing the production. The photochemical oxidation potential is caused by ozone formation in the lower layer of at- mosphere (Haagen-Smit et al., 1953). However, in nature, ozone produce in small quantities but their production has increased in pollutant emission condition and can have adverse effect on human and ecosystems health (Berkner and Marshall, 1965). The value of ozone production affected by the concentration of NOx, the volatile of organic compounds, carbon dioxide and methane which the environmental condition like temperature, radiation and convec- tion (Bare, 2002, 2011).

Also according to Table 4, the value of two environmental in- dicators, acidification and eutrophication potential to produce 1 kg of egg in all production stages were calculated 43/89 g equivalent SO2, 5/42 g equivalent PO4 respectively which in the compare with Dekker et al. (2008) study which declared the value of this indicators for one kg of organic egg 47/8 g equivalent SO2 and 10.9g equivalent PO4 were in lower value. The acidification impact significantly is because of SO2, N20, NH3 emission to air. SO2 at first

produce from the combustion of fossil fuels. However ammonium is alkaline but it oxidized to nitric acid in the atmosphere. Acidifi- cation has adverse effects for plants and animals and also has damaged to buildings and ecosystems (Bare, 2002; Brentrup et al., 2004a, 2004b). Eutrophication including undesirable increasing in biomass production in land and water ecosystems due to arrival of nutrients which can lead to change in combination of plant species (Smith et al., 1999). Eutrophication especially is dangerous in sur- face water because it can amplify the algae growth and finally lead to loss of life in pond and lake (Brentrup et al., 2004a, 2004b).

According to studies, poultry manure is nitrogen-rich, manure management can also give rise to considerable ammonia emissions. Ammonia is not a greenhouse gas itself, but when the ammonia is deposited the nitrogen it contains is added to ecosystems, which means nitrous oxide emissions. This effect is called indirect nitrous oxide emissions (Sonesson et al., 2009).

Based on the information obtained and also calculated the amount of influence inputs on environmental impacts that shown in Table 5, production of poultry feed during the study period had the greatest impact on environmental pollution. Three main crops (corn, wheat and soybean) had the most effect on feed of chicken, So management that performed on the production of them should be going on the right way. The results obtained in this study was consistent with results of similar studies in the field.

20: In farms related to the production of poultry feed, improper use of manure was caused environmental pollution. Ammonia, (NH3), which is very volatile, is formed during storage of manure, both solid manure and slurry. The most effective way to decrease methane and ammonia emissions is to store the manure in a tank, i.e. to not simply cover the store but also have walls (Sonesson et al., 2009).

Poultry manure is a valuable fertilizer with a high content of readily available nitrogen and also a high phosphorus concentra- tion. The chicken manure sometimes has to be dried before being transported away to arable farms. The positive aspect is that the manure is probably spread over a greater area, which can give better nitrogen use efficiency, with lower emissions of nitrous OX- ide and a lower mineral fertilizer requirement (Vestgöte, 2000).

Poultry manure is an interesting substrate for biogas produc- tion. Biodigestion trials on chicken manure show similar biogas yield per ton dry matter as for cattle and pig slurry (Carlsson and Uldal, 2009). The advantage with poultry manure is that it has a high dry matter content, which leads to high biogas yield per ton of manure and makes (Persson and Och Litorell, 2007).

22: In this study, the environmental impacts related to egg pro- duction was performed in Alborz province, Iran. The poultry breeding and egg production is considered as the most the important of agricultural and industrial activities. Since the diet consumption has the most input share in poultry and also ac- cording to obtained information, most of poultries produce and prepare their own diet mixture (combination), so the environ- mental impacts were examined from the cultivation stages of three main production (corn, wheat, soybean) to harvesting, carry to silo's storage and then reaching to poultry (which is considered as an input of poultry) and according to the percent of consumption of each one was examined from the production in diet combination then the value of another input had collected according to poultry and finally the environmental impacts of one kg were determined for 1000 chicken in period of 420 days. According to the results, the value of consumption energy was 30.09 MJ for 1 kg of egg during the all mentioned stages and also global warming or emission of greenhouse gases were evaluated 4.07 kg of CO2 equivalent.
